← all tutorials

System Design Framework — interactive SYSTEM_DESIGN_FRAMEWORK.md system_design_framework.py

How the five building blocks — load balancer, cache, database, message queue, CDN — compose into a request flow, and the back-of-envelope capacity template that decides which block you need. Walk a request through the gate chain, then open the capacity calculator and drag DAU / read-write ratio / payload to watch QPS, bandwidth, and storage recompute live. All numbers match the identical template as system_design_framework.py.

① Component composer — the request gate chain

What to do: each block is a GATE that either satisfies the request or passes it on. Click a gate to mark where the request is satisfied (green). Reads flow LEFT→RIGHT: the further left it's served, the cheaper it is. A CDN hit is ~nothing & <50ms; a DB hit is ~3ms + every gate before it. Toggle the cache to see the DB load vanish.
request type: static asset
WRITES flow the other way — right to the source of truth first, then propagate left: user → LB → app → DB (commit) → CDC/Kafka → cache/CDN update. Read fast (left gate), write safe (right to truth first).

② Capacity calculator — QPS, bandwidth, storage from first principles

What to do: drag the inputs. qps = daily/86400, peak = qps × factor, bandwidth = peak × payload, storage = daily_new × bytes × years. Watch the verdict flip: a single DB handles ~3K QPS; above ~10K read QPS you need a cache; the read egress decides whether a CDN is mandatory.

③ Cache hit rate — the single number that decides shard-or-not

What to do: drag cache hit rate. Origin load = (1 − hit) × read_qps. At 90% hit the DB sees only 10% of reads; at 50% it's near the single-node ceiling. The hit rate decides whether you need one DB or a sharded cluster.
read QPS = 10,000 · single DB ≈ 3,000 QPS

④ Gold check — Design Instagram capacity, recomputed live

What this proves: the page re-runs the full back-of-envelope (50M uploads/day, 500M views/day, 200KB photo, 3x peak, 3yr) on the identical template as system_design_framework.py GOLD CHECK. The green check: OK below confirms the JS numbers match the .py exactly.
check: … JS capacity template matches system_design_framework.py GOLD CHECK (read_peak=17361.1, bandwidth=3.47GB/s, storage=10.95PB, shards=3).
